\section{Introduction}

This is a correct section with no errors. The parameter $\alpha$ controls the learning rate,
and $x_i$ denotes the $i$-th input sample. We use $\mathbf{W}$ to represent weight matrices.

\begin{equation}
  \mathcal{L} = -\sum_{i=1}^{N} y_i \log(\hat{y}_i) + (1 - y_i) \log(1 - \hat{y}_i)
\end{equation}

\section{Typo Errors}

\begin{itemize}
  \item First item with a typo in the command: \hline should be \hline
  \item Another typo: \usepackage is wrong
  \item And: \textbf should be \textbf
\end{itemize}

\section{Math Mode Errors}

The parameters $\alpha$ and $\beta$ control the shape of the distribution.
We can write $x_i$ to denote the $i$-th observation, and $x^2$ for squared values.

\begin{equation}
  x^{ab} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} y_i^2 % FLAGGED: ambiguous fix — could be {x^a}^b instead of x^{ab}
\end{equation}
